name: Phishing Attachment Sandbox Detonation
description: Automated sandbox detonation workflow for suspicious email attachments. Extracts attachment metadata from the phishing alert, submits the file to a sandbox environment for behavioral analysis, and evaluates the detonation verdict. Malicious attachments trigger quarantine of the original email, hash-based blocking across endpoints, and SOC notification. Clean attachments are logged and the alert is closed. Ideal for organizations with sandbox infrastructure for advanced malware detection.
author: T1 Agentics

required_integrations.name: Malware Sandbox
required_integrations.reason: Detonate attachment in isolated sandbox environment for behavioral analysis

required_integrations.name: EDR Platform
required_integrations.reason: Block malicious file hash across all managed endpoints

data.title: Phishing Attachment Alert
data.summary: Triggers on phishing alerts containing file attachments

data.title: Extract Attachment Metadata
data.summary: Parse attachment details (filename, hash, size, MIME type) from the alert

data.title: Submit to Sandbox
data.summary: Detonate attachment in sandbox environment for behavioral analysis

config.message: Submitting {{$.nodes.code-1.result.count}} attachment(s) to sandbox for detonation analysis

data.title: Wait for Detonation
data.summary: Wait for sandbox analysis to complete (up to 5 minutes)

data.title: Malicious Verdict?
data.summary: Check if sandbox detonation returned a malicious verdict

data.title: Quarantine Email

data.title: Notify SOC
data.summary: Alert the SOC with sandbox detonation results and actions taken

config.summary: Malicious attachment confirmed via sandbox. Email quarantined, file hash blocked on EDR, SOC notified.
